concerns.text: Task T025 implements 'compute_stimulus_features.py' to extract video luminance and cut rates. This task has no matching FR/SC in spec.md (FR-001 to FR-008) and is not authorized by the User Stories. It appears to be scope creep introduced by the plan.md but not the spec, leaving the implementer without a requirement to satisfy.

Success Criterion SC-004 requires measuring the 'percentage of epochs retained after ICA' against a 'pre-defined minimum threshold'. No task exists to calculate this specific metric, log the retention rate, or fail the pipeline if the threshold is not met. T016 only handles subject exclusion (>50% rejection), not the global epoch retention metric required by SC-004.

FR-008 requires a sensitivity analysis on the 'gaze variance window size'. Task T027 places this logic inside 'extract_features.py' (label generation). However, sensitivity analysis on a proxy definition to assess robustness of *results* typically belongs in the evaluation/validation phase (US-3) or requires a specific task to vary the window, re-run the model, and compare outcomes. T027 as described conflates label generation with model sensitivity analysis, potentially missing the requirement to validate the *model's* robustness to this parameter change.

Task T027 is tagged with [FR-008] but the description ('vary gaze variance window size and assess robustness') is ambiguous regarding whether it performs the full sensitivity loop (re-training/evaluating) or just calculates a metric. The tag exists, but the task description may not fully cover the 'assess robustness of the results' part of FR-008 if it only modifies the label generation without re-evaluating the model.
